title: dorycnium pentaphyllum subsp. amani
intro: Dorycnium pentaphyllum subsp. amani, also known as Amman's tick clover, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Dorycnium pentaphyllum subsp. amani include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are often used to treat a range of ailments, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as rheumatism and skin problems.
